title: Dimensional shift: sculpting in Figma Draw
key_points: Josh McKenna frames learning a new program as a response to creative block, sharing how it 'saved his practice' after a successful career hit a wall.
He demonstrates a unified Figma Draw workflow with practical techniques for using 2D vector effects to create the illusion of 3D depth.
The talk argues that becoming a student again makes your brain 'porous' to new dimensions, bridging flat design and 3D.
Core thesis: picking up a new tool is not just a technical upgrade but a creative lifeline.
